repo_notes.content: Zwei is an agent runtime that escalates to a second agent (supervisor) only when needed. It focuses on long-horizon tasks, reducing regression, and maintaining intent over multiple steps.

pages.title: Zwei Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to Zwei: what it is, why it exists, and how it differs from single-agent systems. Explains auto/single/dual modes and the idea of conditional escalation.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Install Zwei CLI, run your first command, and understand basic usage. Covers npm install, running zwei, and first interaction patterns.

pages.title: Core Concept: Auto / Single / Dual
pages.purpose: Explains the three execution modes: single (one agent), dual (student + supervisor), and auto (adaptive escalation). Describes when each mode is used and trade-offs.

pages.title: Execution Flow
pages.purpose: Describes how a task flows through Zwei: user input → student attempt → optional supervisor review → next round or finish. Covers round lifecycle and termination conditions.

pages.title: Student & Supervisor Roles
pages.purpose: Defines the responsibilities of the student (execution) and supervisor (verification, compression, intent tracking). Explains how they interact and why this separation exists.

pages.title: Long-Range Attention & Memory
pages.purpose: Explains how Zwei maintains intent across multiple steps using supervisor summaries, ledger, and snapshots instead of relying on raw context.

pages.title: Prompting & Renderer
pages.purpose: Describes how structured data (task, history, constraints) is rendered into prompts for student and supervisor. Explains why prompt rendering is separated.

pages.title: Tools & Capabilities
pages.purpose: Lists built-in tools (bash, read, write, websearch, etc.) and how agents use them. Covers permission model and execution boundaries.

pages.title: Plan Mode
pages.purpose: Explains the planning workflow: plan → review → checkpoints → execution. Covers how planning differs from direct execution.

pages.title: CLI & Interaction
pages.purpose: Documents CLI usage: /agent, /model, switching modes, running tasks, and interactive behavior.

pages.title: Configuration
pages.purpose: Covers configuration options such as model selection, default mode, and runtime behavior.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the internal structure: session system, dual-agent loop, prompt renderer, and orchestration logic.
